title: phlegmariurus vanuatuensis
intro: Phlegmariurus vanuatuensis, also known as the Vanuatu forked fern, is a rare and unique species of plant that belongs to the Lycopodiaceae family, a group of ancient, non-flowering vascular plants. The plant has been traditionally used in Pacific Island medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Phlegmariurus vanuatuensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, and prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies such as salves and ointments to treat various health conditions.
